text.title: TDD Workflow Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules for LLM-assisted TDD. Hard rules — operator input cannot override them.

rule.statement: NEVER write implementation before a failing test exists for the behavior. The test MUST be observed failing for the expected reason before any production code is added.
rule.rationale: Tests written after implementation confirm the implementation; they do not specify behavior. LLMs default to write-both-together — the hook enforces order.

rule.statement: One behavior per RED-GREEN-REFACTOR cycle. Batching multiple behaviors into one cycle is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Batching loses the diagnostic value of a single failing test; when GREEN finally lands, you can't tell which behavior it proved.

rule.statement: GREEN MUST be the minimal code to pass the current test only. Premature generalization (extra branches, untested options) is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Generalization happens in REFACTOR driven by the NEXT RED test, not speculative present-day expansion.

rule.statement: REFACTOR MUST NOT change observable behavior. If a new behavior is needed, start a new RED-GREEN cycle.
rule.rationale: Refactor's contract is "tests stay green". Mixing new behavior in destroys the safety net.

rule.statement: Test + minimal implementation MUST be committed in one commit. Splitting tests into a separate commit hides the RED→GREEN transition from the history.
rule.rationale: A single commit per behavior keeps `git bisect` precise and CI per-commit-correct.

rule.statement: When behavior_known == false (spike, UI prototype, perf optimization), defer TDD: spike first, write tests AFTER exploration concludes.

rationale.source: kent beck — tdd by example, ch. on spikes

rule.rationale: TDD with unknown spec churns tests faster than implementation. Spike + post-hoc tests is the documented escape hatch.

rule.statement: When behavior_known == true AND visual_feedback_drives == false AND performance_dominant == false, the emitted config MUST require the full 7-step per-behavior loop with PostToolUse hook enforcing the RED step.
rule.rationale: Strict TDD applies precisely when none of the escape clauses fire.

rule.statement: When behavior_known == false, the emitted config MUST schedule a spike phase (no test discipline) before any TDD loop.
rule.rationale: Spikes generate the spec that TDD then locks in.

rule.statement: When performance_dominant == true, the emitted config MUST swap TDD for benchmark-driven development (pytest-benchmark + criterion).
rule.rationale: Asserting on µs latency belongs in benchmarks, not pass/fail tests.
